item.title: Resident Evil Veronica announced by Capcom
item.content:encoded: Resident Evil Veronica announced by Capcom - Tuffcub
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil Veronica Reimagines The Classic Entry In The Series in 2027 on PC and Consoles
item.content:encoded:
Following the successful release of Resident Evil Requiem earlier this year, the beloved survival-horror series by CAPCOM will return in 2027 on PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Nintendo Switch 2 with Resident Evil Veronica, a remake of the classic entry in the series originally released on the SEGA Dreamcast back in 2000.

The rumored remake was announced during today's Summer Game Fest showcase just as previously rumored with a deceptive trailer that only showed the modern recreation of Claire Redfield at its very end, alongside the game's name, which will drop "Code" from the original. Unfortunately, a precise release date hasn't been announced outside of a generic 2027, but going by how CAPCOM handled Resident Evil Requiem last year, the game is likely to release in early 2027.

Unfortunately, the Resident Evil Veronica reveal trailer did not show much of the gameplay. Will it lean more towards horror or will CAPCOM attempt to spice the experience up and feature more action-oriented sequences like Resident Evil Requiem did, striking the best balance the series has seen in years? If the game is launching in early 2027, it likely won't take long to learn more about what the next entry will offer in a series that has quickly returned to the glory of the past following the release of Resident Evil 7 Biohazard.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/resident-evil-veronica-reimagines-the-classic-entry-in-the-series-in-2027-on-pc-and-consoles/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Mojang is Stacking Another Game in September 2026 With Minecraft Dungeons 2 Arriving on PC and Consoles
item.content:encoded:
Minecraft Dungeons 2 has a release date, just two months after it was first revealed this past March 2026. It'll arrive on all current-gen PC and console platforms (including Nintendo Switch 2) this coming September 29, 2026. Spotted by Wario64 on X (formerly Twitter), the game's Nintendo eShop page went live earlier than it should've, ahead of its reveal either later today on Summer Game Fest 2026 or on Sunday, during the Xbox Games Showcase.

There's always a few games that entirely leak ahead of the show with an early store page or an early tweet. This year, it's Minecraft Dungeons 2, alongside Hot Wheels Infinite Rush, Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis, Rayman Legends: Retold, and Cronos: Lazarus as either games we knew about getting release dates leaked or unannounced titles getting revealed early.

Both Mojang and Double Eleven are back behind the sequel, which will set you back $29.99 USD if you're not playing it through Xbox Game Pass, which is where you'll almost certainly be able to find it on day one for Game Pass Ultimate subscribers.

The eShop page has since been taken down, though the bits of the description that are visible in the image reveal that the game's campaign will task players to stop an evil chaos across two worlds, further clarifying the small sneak peek we saw in the announcement teaser trailer below.

"Drop the pickaxe, grab a sword, and charge into a thrilling new adventure in Minecraft Dungeons II," the description reads. "Journey through unexplored lands and mysterious locations, in a quest to stop a new evil from causing chaos in not one, but two worlds! Clash with menacing foes in explosive encounters and fight your way through hordes of mobs that will do everything in their power to stop you from saving the day."

Minecraft Dungeons 2 is the latest September 2026 game, as everyone would rather bundle up in one month instead of launching anywhere close to GTA 6, lest it get lost in the craze of over a decade of hype building up for the next game in what is arguably the biggest video game franchise in the world.

The PlayStation State of Play earlier this week revealed several major titles set to arrive in September, and as Wccftech's Alessio Palumbo points out, everyone trying to arrive before say, mid-October, probably isn't going to make things any better than just launching besides GTA 6.

It'll be interesting to see what the games announced during the rest of the Summer Game Fest string of showcases choose to do, and if we see more double-A or triple-A games gamble with September/early October rather than anywhere close to November 2026.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/minecraft-dungeons-2-release-date-leak-summer-game-fest-xbox-games-showcase-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Everything Announced at Summer Game Fest 2026
item.content:encoded:
Sony took center stage earlier this week with the PlayStation State of Play for 2026, but the multi-platform headliner is here now with Geoff Keighley's Summer Game Fest. Set for many a big game and small game to appear as well as many other oddities that are sure to come up (how will the muppets appear this time?) it's time to see what gaming companies have in store for the next year.

Note this is a live announcement recap post. Announcements are being added as they are made, and if you don't see one here yet please refresh the page and it will be there shortly. Additional details may be added as well as trailers. New announcements will be added to the bottom of the post.

Geoff Keighley took to the stage to kick off the Summer Game Fest, celebrating that this was the 7th year of the Summer Game Fest, and that over the next two hours we'd see plenty games over the next two hours. He continued with some Steam stats, like the fact that nearly 10k games released in the first five months of the year, and that there have been a number of 1m+ sellers on Steam, including by small independent developers.

Resident Evil: Veronica Remake revealed

The first game is set in Paris, and has a horror type feel as we enter a weird building, and find the protagonist's brother has made a mess in his Parisian apartment much to his land lady (or property manager?)'s dismay. One attack later, and we begin to see weird things and a montage of various locations and shots as it is revealed to be the long rumored Resident Evil: Veronica remake and a character is revealed as Claire Redfield. Resident Evil: Veronica is releasing in 2027.

Cuphead Sequel in the Works And More

Studio MDHR took the stage next and announced that they were working on a new Cuphead game that will feature hand animation work there. Additionally, there is another small project in the works at the studio, featured on the era that got them into video game. Mighty Cuphead Adventure is a an 8-bit run and gun platformer.

Alien Isolation 2 Announced

At long last, Sega has announced the sequel to the horror classic Alien Isolation, and we will have Alien Isolation 2 coming in the future. This time, the Alien will be roaming an unsuspecting colony world, taking the horror outside of the limits of a space ship.

GenAtlas from Shadow of the Colossus Creator Fumito Ueda Announced

After being teased previously as Project Robot, Fumito Ueda presented his first title in over a decade since The Last Guardian. GenAtlas is being published by Epic Games, and we don't know much more about it other than that it promises to be quite pretty and likely emotionally devastating.

Blood Message Takes the bloody stage

Samurai and others fight in a desperate battlefield as hordes take the field in Blood Message from 24 entertainment. Ride horses, battle hordes, and explore Japan in this intriguing game.

Stranger Than Heaven Release Date Announced

Masayoshi Yokohama of RGG Studio took to the stage next to talk about Stranger Than Heaven, one of their many Japanese-esq life/yakuza games. Featuring a start studded cast including the likes of Snoop Dog in the trailer and Tupac is also in the game. The Stranger Than Heaven release date was announced, and you'll be able to play the game on January 15th, 2027. 

Snoop Dog then joined Yokohama, Geoff, and translator on stage to talk about how he ended up in the game and how the Tupac estate brought him into the game and a lot of others. Snoop Dog also teased we'll hear more about his Death Row Games in the next few months.

Haex is a Strange New Game from a Strange New Studio

From Dead Astronaut, a studio made up of folks who previously worked on titles like The Division and Little Nightmares in Sweden, Hex is a heavily procedural game that you explore and shoot enemies, while solving weird puzzles and uncovering buried things and watch the landscape around you transform from what you did.

Dead by Daylight celebrates its birthday with more horror

Keeping things vague for now, Dead by Daylight issued an invitation to its 10th birthday bash on June 14th.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/everything-announced-at-summer-game-fest-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Summer Game Fest Live 2026: The biggest news, trailers, and announcements
item.content:encoded:
Geoff Keighley’s annual June celebration of games is here. Summer Game Fest Live, the big live show that will feature a whole bunch of gaming news and announcements, begins at 5PM ET and is set to run for two hours. The event itself will take place in person at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles, but it will also be streaming live on YouTube.

Summer Game Fest Live sits squarely in the middle of a big run of video game showcases; PlayStation hosted a State of Play on Tuesday, and the Xbox Games Showcase takes place on Sunday. That doesn’t mean there won’t be some big surprises, though. Last year, for example, Capcom used the show to announce Resident Evil Requiem. According to this year’s livestream’s description, expect a “live look at what’s next in video games, with new game announcements, surprise special guests and more,” and while that’s not very specific, Keighley probably has some flashy news in store.

Read on for our coverage of the biggest updates from the show.

  • Stranger Than Heaven is coming out in January — and features Tupac?

    The next game from RGG Studio will be released on January 15th, 2027, and Tupac is in the game. Didn’t expect that casting.

  • Fumito Ueda’s next game is Gen Atlas.

    Ueda, behind games like Ico, Shadow of the Colossus, and The Last Guardian, showed off his new title, a sci-fi shooter. There’s no details on when it will come out, but it will be available on PS5, Xbox Series X / S, and the Epic Games Store (Epic is publishing it).

  • Uh oh, the xenomorph is back.

    The sequel to one of the scariest games I’ve ever played has been officially unveiled with a brief teaser for Alien Isolation 2. It’s coming to the PS5, Xbox, PC, and even the Switch 2, though there’s no release date yet.

  • Cuphead returns.

    Studio MDHR confirmed that it’s working on a follow-up to Cuphead, though it’s still early in development. Also in the works: an 8-bit platformer set in the same classic animation-inspired universe, which will even be released as Sega Master System cartridge, in addition to modern platforms.

  • Capcom is remaking Resident Evil: Code Veronica.

    Resident Evil: Veronica launches in 2027, and it’s coming to PS5, Xbox Series X / S, Steam, and Nintendo Switch 2.

  • No Highguard-esque ending today.

    After the fiasco surrounding everything Highguard, Geoff Keighley promises that the last Summer Game Fest Live announcement today will be a “single player, narrative game.” The show starts at 5PM ET, so we won’t have to wait too much longer for the big reveal.

  • TGI(SG)F.

    What better way to spend a Friday night than with a deluge of video game trailers and reveals. Summer Game Fest Live kicks off at 5PM ET, and you can watch along live right here.

  • Minecraft Dungeons II could launch this September.

    Perhaps this is a Summer Game Fest Live announcement that got pushed a little early; that event is set to kick off at 5PM ET. (We’ll be watching and covering the show, stay tuned!)

  • Paramount is making a unified games studio.

    Paramount Games Studio puts all of Paramount and Skydance’s development teams under one brand. Weirdly, it will still be making two titles set in Disney universes: Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra and an untitled Star Wars game, Variety reports.

    There will also be a new AAA title announced at Summer Game Fest Live later today.



https://www.theverge.com/games/939484/summer-game-fest-live-2026-biggest-news-trailers-announcements
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Two New Cuphead Games Are On The Way, And One’s An 8-Bit Platform Shooter
item.content:encoded:
Studio MDHR brought puppets to Summer Game Fest this time, alongside the news that it’s making not one, but two brand new Cuphead games…and one’s not what you’d expect.

The first to be announced, with no title or release window to be had, is just “a new, hand-animated Cuphead.” That’s it. We didn’t see anything of it or learn anything more, it’s just going to be another game like Cuphead. Neat! Just, non-specific. It sounds like development may have only started recently, so we might have to wait a while to hear more.

The second game is entitled Mighty Cuphead Adventure, and it’s an 8-bit platform shooter that looks to be treading some of the same ground as the original Cuphead, with some familiar-looking bosses and both Cuphead and Mugman starring. According to a press release, Mighty Cuphead Adventure was actually developed with ’80s technology as a tribute to the games of the era, programmed in classic Assembly Language and designed within the specifications of the Sega Master System. Though it’s coming to modern consoles and PC, there will also be a physical Sega Master System cartridge available for anyone who’s still got one of those consoles lying around. No release window for this one either, though PR says to expect more news in the months to come.

We’ve been wondering what Studio MDHR has been up to since it wrapped up Cuphead, which first came out in 2017 and has since gotten multiple ports and a big DLC expansion entitled The Delicious Last Course, which dropped in 2022. All were very well-received and praised for the hand-drawn animation and commitment to a classic ’30s art style and sound. The game got a TV show, The Cuphead Show!, also in 2022, which was fine.



https://kotaku.com/two-new-cuphead-games-hand-drawn-mighty-cuphead-adventure-2000703098
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Next Big Resident Evil Remake Is Code Veronica
item.content:encoded:
The next big Resident Evil remake isn’t Resident Evil 5. Nope! Today at Summer Game Fest, Capcom announced that it is remaking Resident Evil: Code Veronica. And it is out in 2027.

In the trailer released during the event, the game seems to be first-person. We see Claire Redfield entering a Paris hotel where her brother, Chris, is staying before being grabbed by someone. The game is simply called Resident Evil: Veronica. 

For many Resident Evil fans, Code Veronica is the true Resident Evil 3. It’s set a few months after Resident Evil 2 and was released in 2000 for the Sega Dreamcast. It follows Claire and Chris Redfield as they deal with a virus spreading outside Raccoon City. It was the first Resident Evil game that debuted on a non-PlayStation console, and it also did a lot to expand the scope of the franchise. It’s a very important game, but one which has sometimes felt like a forgotten stepchild. That changes now that, following lots of rumors and fan pleading, Capcom has announced that it is remaking Resident Evil: Code Veronica from the ground up.

It’s the latest Resident Evil remake from Capcom, a publisher that has been on a roll recently. Will this be another big hit for the company? In any case, a lot of Resident Evil sickos are super happy to see Code Veronica finally getting the love it deserves.

Resident Evil: Veronica is launching on consoles and PC in 2027.



https://kotaku.com/the-next-big-resident-evil-remake-is-code-veronica-2000703078
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Alien Isolation 2 Trailer Revealed At Summer Game Fest
item.content:encoded:
A new trailer was shown at Summer Game Fest for Alien Isolation 2, the long-awaited sequel to the survival horror game set in the Alien universe.

No release date or window was revealed, but some new footage showcased the brand new setting, as it breaks the boundaries from a ship to a colony planet.

More Alien Isolation 2 Details

The footage shown during Summer Game Fest was pre-alpha footage, but it revealed that the setting is expanding onto a colony planet, with the frightening Xenomorph continuing to hunt you down.

As expected, it’ll hunt you down whilst lurking in vents and passageways, but with new environments like dark woodlands and crash sites, it’s bound to be a step up from the original, which was released over a decade ago by now.

Check out the trailer here:

This was just one of the many announcements during Summer Game Fest. If you’re looking to stay up to date on everything else, check out our live updates for the showcase here.

Are you excited to dive into the long-awaited sequel for Alien Isolation 2? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the drop off for Nintendo Switch 2 sales in Japan, as well as when the Steam Machine is set to ship.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/alien-isolation-2-trailer-revealed-at-summer-game-fest/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake Release Window Revealed at Summer Game Fest
item.content:encoded:
Capcom made a huge announcement at Summer Game Fest today, revealing the highly anticipated remake of Resident Evil Code: Veronica and its release window.

Resident Evil Code Veronica has been rumored to be in development for quite some time. It is said to be one of two new remakes in the series, alongside Resident Evil Zero. Various details, including release windows and development teams, have been leaked, but players remained uncertain until today, when Capcom finally decided to reveal the information.

Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake Release Set for 2027

The Summer Game Fest panel for Resident Evil Code Veronica Remake provided players with quite a bit of new information. Players got to see enhanced visuals across the board, with higher fidelity and performance across both cutscenes and what seemed like gameplay elements. Towards the end of the trailer, players were also given a release window: the upcoming remake is set to arrive in 2027. Check out the reveal trailer below:

In the story of Resident Evil Code Veronica, players follow Claire Redfield as she continues her search for her brother, Chris Redfield. Her quest leads her to Paris, where she is captured and trafficked to Rockfort Prison. However, the prison comes under sudden attack, allowing Claire to escape, only to discover that the T-virus has broken out and zombies have overrun the facility. With the undead on the loose, she must scavenge for weapons and ammunition to survive. Claire’s journey also takes her to an Antarctic base, where she seeks clues to expose the Umbrella Corporation’s dark motives and uncover the truth behind the mysterious and unsettling Ashford twins.

In other news, here’s everything that was announced during Summer Game Fest 2026. Additionally, a producer on the Resident Evil franchise has hinted that a future game will be set in Japan, a location the series has yet to visit. What are your thoughts on the Resident Evil: Vode Veronica remake’s reveal trailer, and will you be purchasing it when it releases in 2027? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the release date and trailer of a new Hot Wheels game being revealed. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/resident-evil-code-veronica-remake-release-window-revealed-at-summer-game-fest/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New hand-animated Cuphead game and 8-bit action platformer Mighty Cuphead Adventure announced
item.content:encoded:
Two new Cuphead adventures from Studio MDHR.

Studio MDHR has announced two new Cuphead projects: a brand-new, hand-animated Cuphead game early in development, and Mighty Cuphead Adventure, an 8-bit action platformer.

“Craftsmanship is at the heart of everything we do here at Studio MDHR,” said co-founder Chad Moldenhauer in a press release. “When it came time to peel back the curtain and share a little bit about what we’ve been up to, we couldn’t imagine a more fun way to do so than with today’s Special Bulletin. Crafted with the wonderful teams at Toronto’s Stop Motion Department & Continue Agency, we hope the announcement will transport players to the era we’ll be taking them to in our next project, Mighty Cuphead Adventure.”

Here are the first details:

First off, Studio MDHR is happy to officially confirm that development has begun on a brand-new, hand-animated Cuphead video game that will take their beloved characters on a fresh, exciting adventure. This ambitious project is still in its earliest stages of development, and the team looks forward to revealing more at a later date.

On that note, a small team within Studio MDHR has been working in secret on another project: an 8-bit action platformer named Mighty Cuphead Adventure. In true Studio MDHR fashion, this game goes beyond just taking inspiration from the classics of the 1980s… it is actually built with technology of the era! That’s right: Mighty Cuphead Adventure is programmed in classic Assembly Language and designed with the exacting specifications of the Sega Master System home gaming console in mind. While the game will absolutely be compatible with modern consoles and PC, players who want a true blast from the past will be able to experience it on a physical cartridge on the Sega Master System home gaming console.

Studio MDHR will share much more about Mighty Cuphead Adventure in the months ahead, so stay tuned to their social channels for more.

Watch the announcement videos below.

Studio MDHR Special Bulletin

Mighty Cuphead Adventure Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/new-hand-animated-cuphead-game-and-8-bit-action-platformer-mighty-cuphead-adventure-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil: Veronica Is A Code Veronica Remake And It Launches Next Year
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has revealed Resident Evil: Veronica, a remake of Resident Evil – Code: Veronica, and it's launching next year, presumably on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch 2, and PC. It was revealed during today's Summer Game Fest showcase, and it follows Capcom's most recent Resident Evil remake, 2023's Resident Evil 4 remake. 

The original Resident Evil – Code: Veronica is a bit of an in-between game that takes place after Resident Evil 2 and Resident Evil 3: Nemesis (but was released after Nemesis). It originally launched in 2000 and received the HD treatment in 2011. Given Capcom's track record remaking its Resident Evil games of late, this remake wasn't all that unexpected. But it's still exciting nonetheless. And surprise: it looks gorgeous, like all of the other Resident Evil remakes. 

Check it out in the Resident Evil: Veronica reveal trailer for yourself below: 

Resident Evil: Veronica is due out sometime next year. 



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/resident-evil-veronica-is-a-code-veronica-remake-and-it-launches-next-year
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fumito Ueda, legendary developer behind Shadow of the Colossus and Ico, announces single-player epic Gen Atlas for Xbox, PlayStation, and PC
item.content:encoded:
"We hope to share an experience that inspires moments of quiet wonder and discovery.”

Fumito Ueda, the creator of Ico, Shadow of the Colossus and The Last Guardian, revealed his next sci-fi project at tonight's Summer Game Fest - the first big information drop we've seen about the game since its reveal back at The Game Awards 2024.

Developed by Ueda's team at genDESIGN (which also features many other developers that worked with Ueda at Japan Studio), the game is billed as a single-player, open-world, action-adventure, and it's coming to the Epic Games Store, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. The Epic Games Store exclusivity, it must be noted, is in place because the title will be published by Epic itself. There was no release date, nor release window, shared during the presentation.

"Without knowing why, you awaken on an abandoned planet," reads a press release for the game. "Before you lies a vast, silent world. Colossal structures stretch over endless plains, deserted facilities, and an ever-changing sea. The remnants of some grand design litter the planet’s surface.

[video]

"As you journey deeper into this strange world, a colossal robot awaits. Its overwhelming power opens paths to places once beyond reach, transforming your conception of the world. Across an endless expanse of time, the remnants of those forgotten constructs begin to move once more…"

So far, so creepy. You can catch the trailer for the game above, and you won't be surprised to see it thrumming with quiet, melancholic beauty - something we've come to execpt from Ueda over the course of his career.

"The team and I are grateful to all the fans who've been eager to learn more about our game," says Ueda, CEO and creative director of genDESIGN. "Their passion and enthusiasm has always motivated and inspired us. We hope to share an experience that inspires moments of quiet wonder and discovery."

It's been 10 whole years since Ueda's last game, the gorgeous-but-slightly-contentious The Last Guardian on PS4. No longer working under Sony, Ueda and "core staff" at genDESIGN "who led the development of Ico, Shadow of the Colossus, and The Last Guardian" have been independent since the reveal of the company in 2024. "As innovators in the games industry, genDESIGN is committed to creating with uncompromised vision and passion," notes a press release. "Amidst a competitive worldwide landscape, we continually strive to create original games that push boundaries and stand out on a global scale."

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/fumito-ueda-shadow-of-the-colossus-ico-gen-atlas-or-xbox-playstation-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil: Code Veronica Remake announced at Summer Game Fest, coming in 2027
item.content:encoded:
The hunt for Chris begins… again!

Resident Evil: Veronica has been announced at Summer Game Fest. The game will release in 2027 for PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and Nintendo Switch 2.

This reveal came in the form of a trailer, showing some first person guidance through a French apparentment building. While the trailer played coy at first, eventually we saw the reveal of Claire before the title drop.

This game, as fans will have quickly realised, is a remake of Resident Evi; Code Veronica. It follows Claire Redfield as she attempts to track down her brother after the events in Racoon City (pre-explosion), so it fits in a nice gap between mainline games.

This is an interesting turn for the Resident Evil remake series. Many were torn on whether Capcom would follow up Resident Evil 4's remake with a return to Resident Evil 5. However, that games' controversial depiction of Africa raised doubts at the prospect.

So, Code Veronica is a sensible alternative! It's canon, and not so far removed from the mainline story as to be a weird leap for many fans.

It also has big shoes to fill. The Resident Evil 4 remake was the fastest game in the series to hit the ten million sales mark soon after its launch. It was also a critical darling - with our review saying it was "as good as remakes get".

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/resident-evil-code-veronica-remake-summer-game-fest
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Watch: Summer Game Fest 2026 Opening Showcase - Live!
item.content:encoded:
Deep breath, Geoff

Another year, another Geoff Fest. While the showcases may have been rolling in thick and fast this week, tonight is when Summer Game Fest 2026 officially gets underway with its big Opening Showcase.

The event is set to begin in just a few minutes, streamed live from the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood. This year's show promises "two hours of announcements, surprises and more," according to host Geoff Keighley, so let's hope that there's some Switch-relevant stuff in there too.

Here's the exact time that the event will get underway in your region:

  • North America: 2pm PDT / 3pm MDT / 4pm CDT / 5pm EDT
  • UK/Ire: 10pm BST
  • Europe: 11pm CEST / 12am (Sat) EEST
  • Asia/Oceania: 6am (Sat) JST / 5am (Sat) AWST / 7am (Sat) AEST

As for what will make an appearance at the show... we're still not completely sure. Geoff has teased Dead By Daylight, Guild Wars 2, Star Wars Zero Company and Fortnite news over on X (most of which isn't applicable to Switch), but we're sure there will be some surprises thrown in along the way.

We'll be hosting the whole show right here on Nintendo Life. So, if you want to watch along with us, then get comfy, grab some snacks, and let's settle in for the next two hours.



https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/watch-summer-game-fest-2026-opening-showcase-live
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Geoff Keighley Reveals The Final Summer Game Fest Trailer Won’t Be An Online Shooter
item.content:encoded:
Last year’s Game Awards infamously ended with a trailer for Highguard, a live-service free-to-play shooter that didn’t even stick around for three full months before being shut down due to low player count. For Summer Game Fest 2026, show creator and host Geoff Keighley is trying to avoid a repeat of that situation.

On June 5, about an hour before the event was set to kick off, Keighley teased on Twitter that the final reveal of the event wouldn’t be an online live-service multiplayer game like Highguard.

“Today’s final announcement at #SummerGamefest will be a single-player, narrative game,” posted Keighley. “Thank you for the feedback!”

It is for sure a direct reference to the very negative reaction Highguard received from people online in the hours after its initial reveal at the Game Awards 2025. A lot of fans expect the final trailer of these big Geoff events to be something like a new God of War or Last of Us. You know, something big, part of an established franchise, or from a developer with a long track record of hits. And mostly, they don’t want a multiplayer-only character shooter to end the event. It seems Geoff has gotten the memo.

Now, the game begins: What will that final mystery reveal be? What single-player narrative game will Keighley present to fans to wrap up Summer Game Fest 2026?



https://kotaku.com/geoff-keighley-reveals-the-final-summer-game-fest-trailer-wont-be-an-online-shooter-2000703064
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Summer Games Fest Finale Will Be a Single-Player Narrative Title
item.content:encoded:
Geoff Keighley has revealed that the final announcement of Summer Games Fest will be a single-player narrative experience. He announced this on social media, thanking players for their feedback.

This follows the backlash to the final announcement that the 2025 Game Awards would be Highguard. A live service multiplayer game. Of course, Highguard lasted less than 2 months as an active game before being taken offline.

Summer Games Fest Surprises Are Expected

Summer Games Fest will feature plenty of surprises. However, there are certain things known about the show. For example, Paramount Gaming was recently formed, and that studio’s debut game will be shown. Additionally, Star Wars Zero Company will be featured. A gameplay trailer for that title will be shown.

This is the time of year when games take center stage. Sony recently held its PlayStation State of Play presentation. Wolverine was heavily featured, showcasing its blood-filled gameplay. A God of War game featuring Laufey, Kratos’ wife. Check out everything announced as part of that showcase here.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor Summer Games Fest and report any updates as the show airs. Learn more about the failure of Highguard at this link. Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

Minecraft Dungeons 2 Release Date & Price Leaks Early

The price and release date for Minecraft Dungeons 2 have leaked on the Nintendo online store ahead of an official reveal.

Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced

A huge live broadcast with the Summer Game Fest 2026 showcase is set to bring about a flood of news and major reveals for gamers.

Paramount Unveils New Game Studio; First Reveal Coming Today

Skydance Interactive and Skydance New Media have been merged into one to create Paramount Games Studio. Here are more details.

No Rest for the Wicked XBOX Release Delayed Due to Series S

No Rest for the Wicked’s XBOX release has been delayed due to Series S optimization issues. Here are more details.



https://insider-gaming.com/summer-games-fest-finale-will-be-a-single-player-narrative-title/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: LEC Spring Playoffs 2026 Finals Preview: Who Will Lift the Trophy?
item.content:encoded:
The LEC Spring Playoffs 2026 will come to an end this weekend with G2 Esports, Movistar KOI, and Karmine Corp.

EMEA has uncovered its top three League of Legends teams, but only one can lift the Spring 2026 trophy.

G2 Esports have already secured their place in the final and, as a result, have automatically qualified for LoL MSI 2026.

They will await the winners of MKOI and KCorp, who will face off yet again in what will likely be another explosive best-of-five.

Karmine Corp vs Movistar KOI

The rivalry between French and Spanish fans means meetings between these two teams are always heated, and this one will be especially tense with MSI qualification on the line too.

So heated, in fact, that a recent match between the two led to both MKOI and KCorp receiving fines following the LEC Madrid Roadtrip.

Both teams have shown very high highs this year. However, KCorp have to be the slight favourites here.

In every multi-game series between the two teams this year, KCorp has won. Twice in Esports World Cup qualifiers, the aforementioned Spring 2-1, and a close 3-2 in the LEC Versus Playoffs.

Though MKOI have taken games in most of those series, and the team recently pushed G2 to five games in the Upper Bracket final.

MKOI midlaner Joseph Joon ‘Jojopyun’ Pyun is in the form of his career, but he will need his teammates to step up.

How MKOI’s sidelanes each deal with their lane mismatches will likely decide the series.

Can G2 Esports Defend Their LEC Title?

Earlier this year, G2 Esports won LEC Versus to claim the organisation’s 18th LEC/EU LCS title.

They will be favourites to do so, having already defeated both MKOI and KCorp on their way to the final.

Though G2 had to reverse sweep MKOI to get this far, after a disastrous start to the series.

Similarly, in the LEC Versus Final against KCorp, G2 had to rely on a ridiculous play by Rasumus ‘Caps’ Winther at Elder to win the fifth match.

G2 have shown vulnerabilities, but also an x-factor that helps them get out of any situation that other LEC teams haven’t been able to overcome.

And after G2’s First Stand heroics saw them sweep both LCK teams 3-0, hopes for this roster are at an all-time high.

Who do you expect to win the LEC Spring Playoffs? Share your predictions with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/lec-spring-playoffs-2026-finals-preview-who-will-lift-the-trophy/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Minecraft Dungeons 2 Release Date & Price Leaks Early
item.content:encoded:
The store page for Minecraft Dungeons 2 on the Nintendo Switch 2 has seemingly leaked the price and release date for the upcoming sequel.

Originally announced back in March 2026 with a short and brief trailer, it seems like there will be a quick turnaround between announcement and release, with the hack-and-slash title joining an already stacked September line-up.

Minecraft Dungeons 2 Releasing September 29, 2026

It seems like some eagle-eyed fans have noticed that the store page for Minecraft Dungeons 2 has been updated, revealing a September 29, 2026, release date. It joins an already packed September release window, with every game avoiding November for some reason…

Reddit user of the store, alerting fans that Minecraft Dungeons 2’s page update had been made, and lo and behold, if you head to it for yourself, you’ll see the changes clear as day.

Priced at $29.99 USD, a host of other details have been made available, including an estimated game file size of 6.8GB, as well as both local and online four-player multiplayer—as expected based on the original.

To top things off, pre-order details have appeared, with two hero skins, the Twisted cape, and the Twisted chicken pet up for grabs. These leaks have likely arrived ahead of an official reveal during either Summer Game Fest or the XBOX Games Showcase: expect a new trailer in the coming days.

What do you make of the leak? Will you be picking up Minecraft Dungeons 2 during a busy month of September? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the new Paramount game studio and its first game in development, as well as the new Hot Wheels game Infinite Rush whose release date got leaked ahead of a reveal.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/minecraft-dungeons-2-release-date-price-leaks-early/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Is Backrooms The Beginning Of A New Generation Of Hollywood Auteurs, Or Just Another Horror Movie?
item.content:encoded:
In just a week, Backrooms—the movie based on a YouTube series (which, in turn, was based on a 4chan meme)—has become A24’s highest grossing film ever in the United States. Many have heralded it as a breath of fresh air compared to the slurry of sequels and superheroes that have been forced down our throats for the past decade. But it is still, at its core, a horror movie, and it leans on tropes like any other. So, for that matter, is Obsession, the other hit movie directed by a YouTuber making headlines right now. Why does horror have implicit permission to expand its horizons where other genres don’t? On the latest Aftermath Hours, we try to scare up an answer.

This time around, we’re joined by musician, writer, and game developer Liz Ryerson hot on the heels of counterprogramming Summer Game Fest with the second annual Unearthed Treasure Room showcase. The basic idea? SGF is made up of nearly 20 showcases of games yet to come—too many, frankly!—but there are countless already-released games that deserve another shot. We discuss why it’s important to counterprogram something as commercialized as SGF despite a relatively tiny viewership, as well as what games can look like when they’re less constrained by the bounds of specific genres. 

Then we get lost in Backrooms. How does the movie draw on video game aesthetics to create something that feels fresh? And is this new crop of YouTubers turned filmmakers—as also demonstrated by Obsession—the injection of new life Hollywood has been looking for? Or is this another, perhaps slightly more subtle example of popular people and properties being squeezed into tired molds? Lastly, in honor of God Of War’s magnificent new gelatinous cube, we discuss other shapes we’d like to see anthropomorphized in games. 

You can find this week's episode below and on Spotify, Apple, or wherever else you prefer to listen to podcasts. If you like what you hear, leave a review so that we can someday build a massive Aftermath headquarters replete with all sorts of unnerving liminal spaces to frighten away young people.

Here’s an excerpt from our conversation (edited for length and clarity):

Liz: That’s the other narrative that’s going right now: YouTubers are taking over. Because Backrooms is really successful, it’s gonna spawn other [similar] things.

With [Backrooms director] Kane Parsons’ series, he was a YouTuber, but he wasn’t really a YouTuber personality. I think there’s a difference between that and the parasocial stuff with Markiplier; the ideal form of a Markiplier video is one that he’s in and commenting on, whereas Backrooms is Kane basically making a movie version of what he already made on YouTube.

But also, these are all horror movies. At some point, the question needs to be asked: Why do we have to keep shoehorning interesting, novel concepts into horror? That’s the thing with Haunted PS1 as well. There are a lot of interesting game ideas that came out of it, but it keeps having to be shoehorned into horror. You keep having to call things “horror” so that people will actually buy them. That’s been a consistent theme across the years.

Nathan: Why is that, do you think? Why is horror the genre where people are allowed to experiment a little more versus other types of blockbusters and things of that nature?

Liz: It’s just the unknown. It has a built-in appeal. But I don’t think you can call [this moment of YouTubers making popular movies] a flowering of new talent or whatever if they’re just making horror movies. It has to go beyond that.

Chris: Also, everyone talks about this like there’s a renaissance, but name some movies that are as good as… I don’t know—

Liz: L'eclisse? 

Chris: Or even name one that is as good as Texas Chainsaw Massacre. It’s one of the best movies ever made.

A lot of these [modern horror movies] are bad or fine. People are like “Watch Longlegs.” Longlegs isn’t good. Weapons is fine. 

Nathan: To bring things full circle, you wanna know who’s directing a new version of Texas Chainsaw Massacre? Curry Barker, who just directed Obsession.

Chris: Oh, motherfucker. Get away from her!

I don’t know, the reason why horror does well is because you can make it with five dollars, and five million metal guys with goth girlfriends who have Etsy stores will see it. It puts butts in the seats. Like, you’ll go into [a theater where people are seeing Terrifier], and it’s a crowd of the nicest people who are bringing their children, and you’re like “Wow, you guys are really into this. Good luck.”

I’m not anti-horror. I love horror to death. But we’re gonna have to keep lying and saying stuff is horror just to trick people into seeing interesting movies. And it’s depressing a little bit.

Liz: It’s like the endless version of those 1950s movies like The Blob or The Invasion Of The Body Snatchers; it’s considered socially acceptable to deal with whatever taboos or anxieties people have in that form. Even people who are more conservative consider that culturally accessible, whereas stuff that is more confrontational or takes a more explicit approach I think has a much tougher time.

Recommended



https://aftermath.site/podcasts/aftermath-hours-podcast-backrooms-obsession-horror/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
Summer Game Fest 2026 is one of the most intriguing showcases in a while, as the slate of games on the horizon starts to become clear, all while tussling to avoid the looming behemoth of Grand Theft Auto 6.

The PlayStation State of Play showcase earlier this week showed how wide a berth titles are giving to GTA 6, avoiding its November release date, and, as a result, September 2026 and February 2027 are already looking busy—and there are still some big reveals to come.

Unlike State of Play, the Summer Game Fest 2026 showcase features titles across all platforms, and the two-hour show is set to be packed with reveals, world premieres, and plenty more.

All Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase Announcements

As games are shown, they will be updated below in the order they appeared. If something is missing, please let us know in the comments. Trailers will also be added as they become available.

Resident Evil: Veronica

Cuphead Sequel & Mighty Cuphead Adventure

Alien Isolation 2

Gen Atlas

Blood Message

Stranger Than Heaven

Haex

Dead By Daylight

Sand Raiders of Sophie

Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Resynced

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: It's Good To Be 868-BACK
item.content:encoded:
In the past I have had archaic rules about what games I keep on my phone. I used to get sucked into anything I had installed, and at one point I’d spent so much time playing the mobile versions of Settlers and Ticket to Ride that I  became miserable and ruthless to play the games against in real life, having spent countless subway commutes training against computers set to hard.

Eventually I settled on the games of designer Michael Brough: 868-Hack, Imbroglio and mostly P1 Select. The games were nuanced and complex enough to resist simple libidinal addiction but immediate enough to play quickly. All had a similar layout and ethos, and when you instinctively understand the logic of each, the process of playing them feels like Speed Chess. They’re grid-based roguelikes in a more traditional sense of the word, where enemies move when you move. I have spent more time playing P1 Select than some MMOs at this point, and it has become my emergency game when I am stuck in line or at an airport. 

But few games have topped the nuance of Brough’s original hit 868-HACK, and now he’s returned with sequel 868-BACK. Building on the original as well as the PLAN.B DLC, 868-BACK takes the iOS minimalism of the original and builds a cathedral of complexity on it. I believe it to be a masterpiece. 

“I’m in.”

The original 868-HACK is a grid-based game where you play a hacker breaking into a computer system. The setting of the game is intentionally retro, and your character is an abstracted smiley face navigating a 6x6 grid. The walls are shaped like PCBs, and the enemies are goofy little freaks that look like random shapes or if you tried to draw the Martians from The Muppets on an Amiga. 

Each of these enemies is an abstraction of some computing concept: glitches, viruses, daemons. Each moves and acts in a different way: viruses take two moves, daemons take three hits (or clicks in this case) to kill instead of two, glitches can pass through solid blocks. The goal is to progress through the eight stages, collecting as much data in the process, but doing so requires properly arming yourself. You do this by siphoning off data blocks containing powerful programs. The tradeoff is that siphoning alerts more enemies, so the game is ultimately one about figuring out when you have a tactical advantage and pressing that. Usually this takes the form of a choke point and forcing enemies down it, in a process I’ve heard referred to as “waterfalling.” Get to the end of the server alive with enough points and you have won the game.

868-BACK takes this same basic structure and builds outwards. You still start off playing the smiley face hacker, but he is old as hell now, with wrinkles and an eyepatch. Instead of a single server of eight sectors, there are several spanning the course of three weeks. The added complexity necessitates additional tools for survival. You have up to three lives at your disposal, although if you die on the first two servers you fail immediately. Progs still exist and are powered by volts and credits siphoned from the floor, but you begin with one from the jump. Devs (devices) are a new addition, powerful modifiers that are added to your device manager once a server and can be activated one time in each of the eight sectors. Some of those devs are “boostable,” meaning they can be strengthened multiple times over various sectors.  There are also scripts, one time versions of progs that can be used in an emergency. 

Synergy

868-BACK can be brutally hard, but favors people who notice synergies between groups of progs or devs. For example, “.refresh” will cause every enemy to “transmit” again, briefly turning them into the single, one-hit portal they briefly exist as when they enter the server. This dovetails nicely with “.dial,” a low-cost prog that allows the player to end a transmission before it turns into a foe while also warping the player to that location. Low cost progs like “.lag,” “.peek,” “”.swap,” or “.hash” pair nicely with “.combo,” which eliminates enemies based on the number of progs used since you last moved. The prog “.hash” will move enemies of the same type on top of each other, and “.debug” will eliminate any enemies that overlap with objects or enemies. And then there’s “.gravity,” which drops the player and every enemy like a rock, and which dovetails nicely with “.row,” a prog that wipes out everything to the left and the right of the player. I personally found keeping a written log of preferential prog combos to be helpful, and this is not even getting into dev combos, which is where the real pervert synergies lie.

Of course, Brough is not content to simply make the game larger while giving the player more treats. As you progress in difficulty, each new server you hack adds a different level of complexity, dryly positioned as “bonus power ups.” Lethal Stakes makes the current server an all-or-nothing proposition, making you lose the run if you lose that server. Advanced 3D Graphics turns the server into a first person 3D game ALA Faceball 2000. Servers in general will scale up in difficulty, but the game will also give you new nodes to connect to in the form of black markets masquerading as sushi and ramen establishments. 868-BACK is brutal but fair, capricious in what it gives you yet infinitely flexible and precise with what it lets you do. You can be on fire for a while only to burn the whole run with a few poorly-timed clicks. It rewards thinking three steps ahead as well as saying “fuck it, we ball” and making the most reckless choice imaginable. Sometimes, but not always, the two are the same thing.

The dreams of the past

Cyberpunk works best as a stylistic abstraction. What you imagined hacking was in your youth was never even close to being real, lines of dull text and credit card fraud standing in contrast to the fashion and brightly-colored sprites of the Hackers. 868-BACK is playfully aware of the age of what it references, and even the representation of yourself is visibly old. When you die the first time, a cutscene plays of a steel claw descending on your character, hunched over alone in their apartment. The game is that old dream of dialup turned into an abstraction tighter than a steel trap. It is Number Munchers and Rouge fused with Drexciya, a deep well that keeps going deeper if you let it. Brough’s games have earned an eternal place on my phone, and 868-BACK has now earned that place on my Steam Deck. You can, and should, get lost in these servers forever.



https://aftermath.site/868-back-review-michael-brough/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Wizards of the Coast Addresses Some Elements of Controversial D&D Beyond Drops
item.content:encoded:
Wizards of the Coast is making changes to how content will be released on D&D Beyond. In response to player feedback and critiques, the company will be revising how D&D Beyond drops will be handled for users on the platform, providing more options for looking at the material.

The Changes to D&D Beyond Drops

In an official post on D&D Beyond, Wizards of the Coast addressed the concerns voiced by the community regarding D&D Beyond drops. For those not aware, the platform started releasing small content drops in May for users who had a Hero or Master tier subscription. These drops were small, providing things like monster statblocks or new spells.

On paper, this isn't an inherently bad idea for ongoing TTRPG support in the digital age. The problem is the way the program was implemented lead to two key issues identified in the post. First, was the inability to content share. If you had a Master tier subscription on D&D Beyond, any books and supplementary material you had bought on the platform could be shared to players in your games. Everything...except the material in D&D content drops.

This is considered an oversight by Dungeons & Dragons players given the conventional wisdom behind TTRPG resources. Generally, new books and supplements are bought by the GM then shared among players. This is true either in physical tables or on VTT platforms. The fact that all content drops were paywalled in this way just adds several unnecessary steps to the play experience.

Wizards of the Coast doesn't have an exact solution to content sharing at this time, but said an update in this matter will be available in the upcoming weeks.

The second issue addressed with D&D Beyond drops is how exactly will this material become available to non-subscribers. On this front, WOTC has confirmed that every May will see a D&D Beyond drop annual bundle, giving all users the chance to buy an entire year's worth of material in one resource on the marketplace.

Generally, this is not a bad solution. R. Talsorian Games, the studio behind Cyberpunk Red, has a similar model with ongoing support with their dark sci-fi TTRPG. Free material gets released as DLC digitally on their websit before getting physically published in softcover Interface books, allowing people who want physical materials to have them. At the time of writing, it is unclear if the content drop bundles will get a similar physical release.

At the time of writing, the second month of D&D Beyond drops has arrived. The drop includes several Corrupted Angel statblocks, five horror-themed battlemaps, three new spells, Searing Orb, Tortoise Shell, and Void Star, and three unique magic items: Amulet of Retributive Healing, Eternal Chalk, and Salubrious Armor.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/wizards-of-coast-addresses-some-elements-of-controversial-dd-beyond-drops
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Robot Kicks Small Child In Stomach During Event
item.content:encoded:
Folks, I’m sorry to report that, once again, the robots are up to some bullshit. This time, it involves a humanoid robot wearing a blue clown wig who was recorded kicking a small child right in the gut, causing the young kid to double over in pain as other children nearby laughed.

As reported by Futurism, the incident of robot violence occurred earlier this week during an event in Xinjiang, China. The Shanghai Daily newspaper reported that the young boy was not seriously hurt by the kick, but the mother claimed staff were “slow to react” after the bot attack.

You know who wasn’t slow to react? The kids around the poor boy who started giggling and laughing. Cold, but I have to admit, if I were there and much younger, I too would laugh my ass off at a friend or classmate getting roundhouse-kicked by a robot in a clown wig. Heck, hearing that the kid is okay and doing well, I’ll admit I snickered a bit watching the clip again. That kid just folds up after the kick. Damn. That robot wasn’t holding back.

Of course, if you’ve been reading Kotaku in the past, you’ll be well aware that this isn’t the first time we’ve seen robots go wild and be shitty. In fact, it’s become a bit of a beat around these parts. Somehow, I became the guy who covers robots doing dumb shit or failing in spectacular ways. Not sure how that happened, but hey, I’ll take it.

Anyway, I can’t wait for the next sign of the upcoming robot uprising. Sure, we’ll all perish under the steel feet of our robotic overlords one day, but at least we get to enjoy some silly videos between now and then. That’s something, right?



https://kotaku.com/robot-kicks-small-child-in-stomach-during-event-2000703013
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Everything Coming in Rocket League Season 23
item.content:encoded:
Rocket League Season 23 is coming soon and is going to be themed around the upcoming football World Cup.

Epic Games and Psyonix have released full details about what Rocket League fans can look forward to in the upcoming update.

There is a lot of buzz surrounding the game following the RLCS Paris Major and the announcement of Unreal Engine 6.

As well as World Cup cosmetics, the update is also bringing huge quality-of-life improvements to the game, including side-selection after a demo.

Here is everything new coming in Rocket League Season 23.

Rocket League Season 23 Release Date

Rocket League Season 23 will release on Wednesday, June 10 at 9:00 AM PT (12:00 PM EDT).

Rocket League World Cup Content

Unlike EA Sports FC 26’s World Cup mode, Epic Games has secured the FIFA World Cup 2026 licensing for Rocket League.

The Rocket League x FIFA World Cup 2026 limited-time event (LTE) will run until July 20, 9 AM PT.

It will also see the release of a World Cup-themed Arena Variant called United Futura.

Once Season 23 releases, players will be able to pick their nation by July 13 9:00 AM PT (12:00 PM EDT) to claim a free Octane Decal and Title.

This decal will track players’ performances for a global leaderboard.

Once the World Cup ends, every player will receive a “World Cup Champion” Player Title, with the winning country’s flag, and all 47 other World Cup Decals.

Players who played for the winning country will also receive a gold variant of the title.

Additionally, LTE Challenges will reward a Goal Explosion, a Boost, and a universal Decal celebrating the football tournament.

Completing 10 out of the 14 World Cup Challenges will then unlock the Hyundai Ioniq 6 Car Body.

Rocket League Quality of Life Upgrades

As well as the World Cup content, Psyonix is also adding a ton of quality-of-life improvements to the 10-year-old game.

In possibly one of the biggest changes ever, Rocket League will now allow players to choose what side they respawn on.

The total list of new features coming in Rocket League Season 23 are:

  • Demo Respawn Control
  • Custom Training Velocity and Car Pitch, and Local Starting Boost Control
  • Ball and Car Speed Graphs
  • Streamer Anonymizer
  • Hitbox Visualization
  • Auto-Skip Goal Replays
  • Improved Friend Presence to include score and time remaining 
  • Goal Scorer’s View in Replays

Rocket League Jynxzi Event

Rocket League is also partnering with content creator Nicholas ‘Jynxzi’ Stewart for a mini event.

Those who end up in a match with the popular streamer will receive Jynxzi-themed cosmetics.

If you win on the same team as Jynxzi, you’ll get the Dad’s Reinforced Chair. Defeat him, and you’ll get the Jynxzi’s OG Chair Topper.

Win or Lose, you’ll also receive the BAZALRIGHT Decal. 

Rocket Pass Premium

Season 23’s Rocket Pass Premium will unlock two new vehicles:

  • •Ryza Trophy (Octane Hitbox) – first unlock
  • •Ryza T60  (Breakout Hitbox) – in the higher Tiers

The Pass also features new Boosts, new Trails, fresh Antennas, and Player Anthems built for the season.

What other features would you like to see added to Rocket League? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And relive the Paris Major Grand Final as Vatira furthered his claim of Rocket League GOAT.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.
